name: Lemongrass Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice thin the bottom 8 inches of the lemongrass stalks.
2. Place the sliced lemongrass in a very clean 1-quart glass jar and bruise it with a wooden spoon.
3. Add the rice vinegar to the jar.
4. Cover the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 4 days up to 2 weeks, depending on the desired strength of flavor.
5. Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a clean glass pitcher, discarding the lemongrass slices.
6. Pour the strained vinegar into 2 very clean half-pint glass jars.
7. Seal the jars with lids.
Once steeped, this vinegar will keep for several months. (Garnishes and other solids left in the jar may discolor or break down.)
